name: Antigua and Barbuda

colors.symbolism: the African heritage of the people; the rich soil of the islands
hope; also the sea around the islands
the white sand beaches of the islands
the rising sun, marking a new era

description: The flag has a stylised inverted-V shape: a black band across the upper portion bears a yellow half-sun with rays rising over a narrow blue stripe and a white stripe; below, two red triangles run from the upper hoist and upper fly down to a single point at the bottom centre, meeting along the flag's vertical axis.
history: Adopted on 27 February 1967 when the islands became an Associated State of the United Kingdom, and retained when full independence was achieved on 1 November 1981. Designed by Sir Reginald Samuel, an Antiguan artist and sculptor, whose entry was chosen from over six hundred submissions in a national competition. The V-shape was intended to evoke victory and the rising sun a new era for the people.

features.symbolism: an inverted-V (a pile with its base along the top edge and its apex at the bottom centre) contains horizontal bands of black, blue and white; the two outer red triangles surround the pile and represent the dynamism of the people. The shape evokes both victory (V) and a rising sun on the new horizon
the bands inside the pile: black for the African heritage of the people and the rich soil, blue for hope and the sea, white for the white sand beaches

features.role: rising sun on the black band
features.symbolism: the dawning of a new era; the rising sun also recalls the country's tropical climate
